negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: The audited code does not manually read executor from word 0, encrypted_secrets from word 1 offset, and ttl from word 2 with explicit zero-offset empty-secrets and bounds/overflow errors.
related_but_insufficient_code: The only port ExecutorRequest decoder delegates to ethabi::decode over a fixed ParamType list and token extraction, so it does not expose explicit word/offset/length parsing branches.
Searches of audited dependency repos found no alternate manual ExecutorRequest decoder implementing zero-offset empty encrypted_secrets handling.

notes: Confirmed by exact searches for manual ABI parser helpers and InputTooShort/zero-offset handling.
rationale: The primary diff replaces tuple ABI decoding with explicit word, offset, length, bounds, and ttl parsing logic for ExecutorRequest. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: ExecutorRequest decoding should read the executor address from the first ABI word, encrypted_secrets from the dynamic bytes offset in the second word, and ttl from the third word, including support for zero-offset empty encrypted secrets and explicit bounds/overflow errors.
suggested_falsification_tests: Feed ExecutorRequest::decode valid ABI data with a right-aligned executor address, dynamic encrypted_secrets bytes, and ttl, plus a zero-offset empty-secrets payload, and assert successful decoding; also assert malformed offsets, lengths, or overflowing ttl return the documented codec errors.
summary: ExecutorRequest decoding is not the manual ABI word parser required by this invariant.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Feed a zero-offset empty-secrets payload and malformed offset/length cases to the current decoder; it should not exercise the invariant-specified manual parsing behavior.
title: Executor requests decode ABI fields manually
